rulings.Case Title: RANDALL RADFORD VS. BAE SYSTEMS SAN FRANCISCO SHIP REPAIR, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Dismiss Or In The Alternative, Disqualify Pltff'S Counsel Due To Misconduct
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, June 5, 2014, Line 2, DEFENDANT BAE SYSTEMS SAN FRANCISCO SHIP REPAIR, INC's Motion To Dismiss Or In The Alternative, Disqualify Plaintiff's Counsel Due To Misconduct The motion to dismiss is denied. "California courts have inherent power to terminate litigation for deliberate and egregious misconduct when no other remedy can restore fairness¿" Stephen Slesinger, Inc. v. Walt Disney Co. (2007) 155 Cal.App.4th 736, 761. In Stephen Slesinger, Inc., the plaintiff whose case was dismissed engaged in criminal trespass, stole confidential documents, and altered documents with the intent to mislead. The alleged misconduct of plaintiff's counsel in this case does not rise to that level and dismissal is not warranted. The Court denies the motion to disqualify. "If the status or misconduct which is urged as a ground for disqualification will have a continuing effect on the judicial proceedings which are before the court, it is justified in refusing to permit the lawyer to participate in such proceedings." Chronometrics, Inc. Sysgen, Inc. (1980) 110 Cal.App.3d 597, 607. Defendant's motion is largely based on speculation and supposition. The Court does not have a basis to know whether the jury pool has reviewed the cited material or whether jurors have been tainted before a jury panel has been summoned and questioned. The trial judge supervising voir dire will be in the best position to determine if there is a problem and, if so, how best to address it. The trial judge will also be able to issue and enforce motions in limine that can control and avoid prejudicial conduct. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/pjb

rulings.Case Title: RANDALL RADFORD VS. BAE SYSTEMS SAN FRANCISCO SHIP REPAIR, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, June 5, 2014, Line 2, DEFENDANTS BAE SYSTEMS SAN FRANCISCO SHIP REPAIR, INC. AND HUGH VANDERSPEK'S Motion For Preliminary Injunction The Court denies defendants' motion for preliminary injunction and/or asking the Court to exercise its inherent power to control proceedings before it. Defendants have at most shown a possibility of prejudice, which is insufficient to demonstrate that a prior restraint is warranted in this case. As stated in the order denying dismissal/disqualification, the Court does not now know whether there is prejudice caused by counsel's websites/statements. "Where a party contends his or her right to a fair trial has been or will be compromised by pretrial publicity, the law has long imposed on that party the burden of producing evidence to establish the prejudice. '[I]t is not enough for a court to decide that the fair trial right may be affected by the exercise of free speech.'" Hurvitz v. Hoefflin (2001) 84 Cal.App.4th 1232, 1242. It is premature to determine whether the jury pool has been tainted. Juror admonitions and instructions are presumptively adequate means to address the threat of jury contamination. Steiner v. Sup. Crt. (2013) 220 Cal.App.4th 1479, 1487. The trial judge supervising voir dire will be in the best position to determine if there is a problem and, if so, how best to address it. Although the Court is not granting the requested relief, the Court expects all parties and counsel to abide by their legal and ethical obligations before and during trial. Again, the trial judge will be in the best position to determine what if any redress might be appropriate for any misconduct that may occur. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/PJB

rulings.Case Title: MILLICENT J. BROWN VS. YELLOW CAB COOPERATIVE, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION And Notice Of Motion
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, June 5, 2014, Line 13, DEFENDANTS MANOJ SHRESTHA, YELLOW CAB COOPERATIVE, INC. DBA YELLOW CAB'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION And Notice Of Motion Granted. Plaintiff may not seek punitive damages. Defendants met their initial burden by producing evidence establishing that the driver was not acting with malice, oppression, or fraud. (Defendants' Undisputed Material Fact "UMF" nos. 1-4.) Plaintiff has not raised a triable issue of material fact. First, although Plaintiff argues that the driver acted intentionally, the complaint only states causes of action for negligence. The pleadings serve as the "outer measure of materiality" in a summary judgment motion, and the motion may not be granted or denied on issues not raised by the pleadings. (Laabs v. City of Victorville (2008) 163 Cal.App.4th 1242, 1258.) In any event, Plaintiff has not presented any evidence from which a jury could reasonably conclude by clear and convincing evidence that the driver intended to injure Plaintiff. Second, Plaintiff has not introduced evidence establishing that the driver was otherwise acting with malice, oppression, or fraud. The case cited in support of Plaintiff's argument that the driver acted with a conscious disregard of the safety of others, Peterson v. Superior Court (1982) 31 Cal.3d 147, is factually distinguishable. In Peterson, there were allegations that the driver of the vehicle was drunk. Here, it is undisputed that the driver was using a legal, hands-free Bluetooth device. (UMF no. 4.) This is insufficient to raise a triable issue of material fact as to whether the driver was acting with a conscious disregard of the safety of others. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/PJB

rulings.Case Title: CACH, LLC VS. AGUSTIN VAZQUEZ et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION / Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Or Preclude Bill Of Particulars (Discovery)
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, June 5, 2014, Line 3, DEFENDANT AGUSTIN VAZQUEZ's Motion To Compel Or Preclude Bill Of Particulars (Discovery). Pro Tem Judge Rebecca Woodson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant Agustin Vasquez's Motion to Compel or Preclude Bill of Particulars is GRANTED. Moving party to submit Order to Court for signature. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rwoodson@mckennalong.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: HENRY M BURGOYNE III VS. GOLDSTERINRIGHT, A CALIFORNIA PROFESSIONAL CORPOR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, June 5, 2014, Line 9, DEFENDANTS CALEGARI & MORRIS, ACCOUNTANTS, A PROFESSIONAL AND MONIQUE TIGER, C.P.A'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT The motion to strike is granted with leave to amend as to the claim for punitive damages in light of the Court's ruling on the demurrer as to claims that could support punitive damages. The motion to strike is granted without leave to amend as to the prayer for attorney's fees incurred in litigating this action. Plaintiff fails to plead a statutory, legal, or contractual basis for such an award. The tort of another doctrine does not apply to allow recovery of such fees in this case. This ruling is without prejudice to Plaintiff attempting to establish a right to recover other attorney's fees incurred elsewhere as part of a substantive damages claim. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. rulings.Case Title: HENRY M BURGOYNE III VS. GOLDSTERINRIGHT, A CALIFORNIA PROFESSIONAL CORPOR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, June 5, 2014, Line 9, DEFENDANTS CALEGARI & MORRIS, ACCOUNTANTS, A PROFESSIONAL AND MONIQUE TIGER, C.P.A'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT The demurrer to the professional negligence cause of action is overruled. Plaintiff alleges sufficient facts to create a duty of care owed to him under Biakanja v. Irving (1958) 49 Cal.2d 647, 650 since the assignment covered determining his personal interest. The demurrer to the breach of fiduciary duty cause of action is sustained with leave to amend. The relationship between an accountant and client is ordinarily not a fiduciary one. "A fiduciary relationship is created where a person reposes trust and confidence in another and the person in whom such confidence is reposed obtains control over the other person's affairs." Lynch v. Cruttenden & Co. (1993) 18 Cal.App.4th 802, 809. Plaintiff does not allege sufficient facts showing how defendant obtained control over plaintiff's affairs or otherwise became a fiduciary. The demurrer to the breach of oral contract cause of action is overruled because plaintiff sufficiently alleges that he is an intended third party beneficiary of the oral agreement. The demurrer to the fraud/concealment cause of action is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff has leave to plead facts showing that he relied on defendant's alleged misconduct to his detriment and was damaged by that reliance. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
